Integration Tests\n\nThis directory contains integration tests for Miroir that run against a real Docker Compose stack.\n\n## Prerequisites\n\nStart the Docker Compose development stack:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml up -d\n```\n\nWait for all containers to be healthy (may take 30-60 seconds):\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml ps\n```\n\n## Running Tests\n\nRun all integration tests:\n\n```bash\ncargo test --test integration -- --test-threads=1\n```\n\nRun a specific test:\n\n```bash\ncargo test --test integration test_document_round_trip -- --exact\n```\n\nRun tests with output:\n\n```bash\ncargo test --test integration -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture\n```\n\n## Test Coverage\n\n| Test | Description | AC Reference |\n|------|-------------|--------------|\n| `test_document_round_trip` | Index and retrieve 1000 documents | §8.1 |\n| `test_search_shard_coverage` | Verify search hits all 16 shards | §8.2 |\n| `test_facet_aggregation` | Verify facet counts sum correctly | §8.3 |\n| `test_offset_limit_paging` | Test pagination with offset/limit | §8.4 |\n| `test_settings_broadcast` | Verify settings propagate to all nodes | §8.5 |\n| `test_task_polling` | Test task status polling | §8.6 |\n| `test_health_check` | Verify health endpoint responds | §8.7 |\n| `test_direct_meilisearch_access` | Verify direct node access for debugging | §8.7 |\n| `test_node_failure_rf2` | Test graceful degradation with node failure | §8.8 |\n\n## RF=2 / High Availability Tests\n\nThe `test_node_failure_rf2` test requires the RF=2 docker-compose stack:\n\n```bash\n# Start the RF=2 stack (6 Meilisearch nodes, RF=2, RG=2)\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev-rf2.yml up -d\n\n# Run the node failure test\ncargo test --test integration test_node_failure_rf2 -- --ignored\n\n# Clean up\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev-rf2.yml down -v\n```\n\nThe node failure test:\n1. Indexes documents with RF=2 (each document replicated to both replica groups)\n2. Stops a Meilisearch node mid-test (`docker stop miroir-meili-1`)\n3. Verifies that searches still work using remaining replicas\n4. Restarts the node and verifies recovery\n\n## Cleanup\n\nStop and remove containers:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml down -v\n```\n\nFor RF=2 tests:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev-rf2.yml down -v\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Containers not starting\n\nCheck container status:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml ps\n```\n\nCheck logs:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml logs miroir\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml logs meili-0\n```\n\n### Port conflicts\n\nIf ports 7700-7703 are already in use, modify the port mappings in `examples/docker-compose-dev.yml`.\n\nFor RF=2 tests, ensure ports 7710-7716 and 6379 are available.\n\n### Tests failing with connection refused\n\nEnsure the Docker Compose stack is running:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml ps\n```\n\nWait for all containers to be healthy before running tests.\n\n### Node failure test not working\n\nThe RF=2 test requires:\n1. The RF=2 docker-compose stack to be running\n2. Sufficient memory for 6 Meilisearch containers + Redis\n3. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_a84394cb9fa94a91939ea673","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Integration tests for Miroir with docker-compose.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! These tests run against a real Miroir + Meilisearch stack started via docker-compose.\n4\t//! Tests cover:\n5\t//! - Document round-trip (1000 docs)\n6\t//! - Search coverage across all shards (unique-keyword test)\n7\t//! - Facet aggregation\n8\t//! - Offset/limit paging\n9\t//! - Settings broadcast\n10\t//! - Task polling\n11\t//! - Node failure with RF=2\n12\t//!\n13\t//! Run with:\n14\t//!   cargo test --test integration -- --test-threads=1\n15\t//!\n16\t//! Prerequisites:\n17\t//!   docker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml up -d\n18\t\n19\tuse serde_json::json;\n20\tuse std::collections::HashSet;\n21\tuse std::time::Duration;\n22\t\n23\t/// Base URL for Miroir API (from docker-compose)\n24\tconst MIROIR_BASE_URL: &str = \"http://localhost:7700\";\n25\t\n26\t/// Master key for authentication (from dev-config.yaml)\n27\tconst MASTER_KEY: &str = \"dev-key\";\n28\t\n29\t/// HTTP client for making requests\n30\t#[derive(Clone)]\n31\tstruct HttpClient {\n32\t    client: reqwest::Client,\n33\t    base_url: String,\n34\t    master_key: String,\n35\t}\n36\t\n37\timpl HttpClient {\n38\t    fn new() -> Self {\n39\t        let client = reqwest::Client::builder()\n40\t            .timeout(Duration::from_secs(30))\n41\t            .connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(5))\n42\t            .build()\n43\t            .expect(\"Failed to create HTTP client\");\n44\t\n45\t        Self {\n46\t            client,\n47\t            base_url: MIROIR_BASE_URL.to_string(),\n48\t            master_key: MASTER_KEY.to_string(),\n49\t        }\n50\t    }\n51\t\n52\t    async fn get(&self, path: &str) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n53\t        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", self.base_url, path);\n54\t        let response = self.client\n55\t            .get(&url)\n56\t            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n57\t            .send()\n58\t            .await\n59\t            .map_err(|e| format!(\"GET {} failed: {}\", url, e))?;\n60\t\n61\t        let status = response.status().as_u16();\n62\t        let body = response.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to read body: {}\", e))?;\n63\t        Ok((status, body))\n64\t    }\n65\t\n66\t    async fn post(&self, path: &str, body: &serde_json::Value) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n67\t        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", self.base_url, path);\n68\t        let response = self.client\n69\t            .post(&url)\n70\t            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n71\t            .header(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n72\t            .json(body)\n73\t            .send()\n74\t            .await\n75\t            .map_err(|e| format!(\"POST {} failed: {}\", url, e))?;\n76\t\n77\t        let status = response.status().as_u16();\n78\t        let body = response.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to read body: {}\", e))?;\n79\t        Ok((status, body))\n80\t    }\n81\t\n82\t    async fn patch(&self, path: &str, body: &serde_json::Value) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n83\t        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", self.base_url, path);\n84\t        let response = self.client\n85\t            .patch(&url)\n86\t            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n87\t            .header(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n88\t            .json(body)\n89\t            .send()\n90\t            .await\n91\t            .map_err(|e| format!(\"PATCH {} failed: {}\", url, e))?;\n92\t\n93\t        let status = response.status().as_u16();\n94\t        let body = response.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to read body: {}\", e))?;\n95\t        Ok((status, body))\n96\t    }\n97\t\n98\t    async fn delete(&self, path: &str) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n99\t        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", self.base_url, path);\n100\t        let response = self.client\n101\t            .delete(&url)\n102\t            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n103\t            .send()\n104\t            .await\n105\t            .map_err(|e| format!(\"DELETE {} failed: {}\", url, e))?;\n106\t\n107\t        let status = response.status().as_u16();\n108\t        let body = response.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to read body: {}\", e))?;\n109\t        Ok((status, body))\n110\t    }\n111\t}\n112\t\n113\t/// Wait for a task to complete by polling the task endpoint\n114\tasync fn wait_for_task(client: &HttpClient, index_uid: &str, task_uid: u64, timeout_secs: u64) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String> {\n115\t    let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n116\t    let timeout = Duration::from_secs(timeout_secs);\n117\t\n118\t    while start.elapsed() < timeout {\n119\t        let (status, body) = client.get(&format!(\"/tasks/{}\", task_uid)).await?;\n120\t        if status == 200 {\n121\t            if let Ok(task) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&body) {\n122\t                if let Some(status_str) = task.get(\"status\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n123\t                    if status_str == \"succeeded\" {\n124\t                        return Ok(task);\n125\t                    } else if status_str == \"failed\" {\n126\t                        return Err(format!(\"Task failed: {}\", body));\n127\t                    }\n128\t                }\n129\t            }\n130\t        }\n131\t        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n132\t    }\n133\t\n134\t    Err(format!(\"Task {} timed out after {} seconds\", task_uid, timeout_secs))\n135\t}\n136\t\n137\t/// Generate test documents with unique keywords for shard coverage testing\n138\tfn generate_test_documents(count: usize) -> Vec<serde_json::Value> {\n139\t    (0..count).map(|i| {\n140\t        json!({\n141\t            \"id\": i,\n142\t            \"title\": format!(\"Document {}\", i),\n143\t            \"keyword\": format!(\"keyword_{}\", i % 16), // 16 unique keywords for 16 shards\n144\t            \"color\": [\"red\", \"green\", \"blue\"][i % 3], // For facet testing\n145\t            \"score\": i % 100,\n146\t            \"shard_hint\": format!(\"shard_{}\", i % 16), // Help verify shard distribution\n147\t        })\n148\t    }).collect()\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t/// Test 1: Document round-trip (1000 docs)\n152\t#[tokio::test]\n153\tasync fn test_document_round_trip() {\n154\t    let client = HttpClient::new();\n155\t\n156\t    // Create index\n157\t    let create_body = json!({\n158\t        \"uid\": \"round_trip_test\",\n159\t        \"primaryKey\": \"id\"\n160\t    });\n161\t\n162\t    let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes\", &create_body).await.unwrap();\n163\t    assert!(status == 202 || status == 200, \"Index creation failed: {}\", body);\n164\t\n165\t    let task_uid: u64 = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&body)\n166\t        .unwrap()\n167\t        .get(\"taskUid\")\n168\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n169\t        .unwrap();\n170\t\n171\t    wait_for_task(&client, \"round_trip_test\", task_uid, 30).await.unwrap();\n172\t\n173\t    // Index 1000 documents\n174\t    let docs = generate_test_documents(1000);\n175\t    let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes/round_trip_test/documents\", &json!(docs)).await.unwrap();\n176\t    assert_eq!(status, 202, \"Document indexing failed: {}\", body);\n177\t\n178\t    let task_uid: u64 = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&body)\n179\t        .unwrap()\n180\t        .get(\"taskUid\")\n181\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n182\t        .unwrap();\n183\t\n184\t    wait_for_task(&client, \"round_trip_test\", task_uid, 60).await.unwrap();\n185\t\n186\t    // Verify all 1000 documents are retrievable by ID\n187\t    for i in 0..1000 {\n188\t        let (status, body) = client.get(&format!(\"/indexes/round_trip_test/documents/{}\", i)).await.unwrap();\n189\t        assert_eq!(status, 200, \"Failed to fetch document {}: {}\", i, body);\n190\t\n191\t        let doc: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&body).unwrap();\n192\t        assert_eq!(doc.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_i64()), Some(i64::try_from(i).unwrap()));\n193\t        assert_eq!(doc.get(\"title\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()), Some(format!(\"Document {}\", i).as_str()));\n194\t    }\n195\t\n196\t    // Clean up\n197\t    let _ = client.delete(\"/indexes/round_trip_test\").await;\n198\t}\n199\t\n200\t/// Test 2: Search covers all shards (unique-keyword test)\n201\t#[tokio::test]\n202\tasync fn test_search_shard_coverage() blue_count = facet_distribution.get(\"blue\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(0);\n357\t\n358\t    let total = red_count + green_count + blue_count;\n359\t    assert_eq!(total, 100, \"Expected Vec::new();\n414\t\n415\t    for page in 0..5 {\n416\t        let offset = page * page_size;\n417\t        let search_body = json!({\n418\t            \"q\": \"\",\n419\t            \"limit\": expected_count = if page < 4 { page_size } else { 0 };\n438\t        if page < 4 {\n439\t            assert_eq!(hits.len(), expected_count, \"Expected {} results on page {}, got {}\", expected_count, page, hits.len());\n440\t        }\n441\t    }\n442\t\n443\t    // Verify we got all 100 IDs and they're unique and sequential\n444\t    assert_eq!(all_ids.len(), 100, \"Expected 100 total documents, client.delete(\"/indexes/paging_test\").await;\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t/// Test 5: Settings broadcast\n452\t#[tokio::test]\n453\tasync fn test_settings_broadcast() {\n454\t    let client = Meilisearch node access (for debugging)\n578\t#[tokio::test]\n579\tasync fn test_direct_meilisearch_access() {\n580\t    let client = HttpClient::new();\n581\t\n582\t    // Access Meilisearch node 0 directly\n583\t    let client = reqwest::Client::builder()\n584\t        .timeout(Duration::from_secs(5))\n585\t        .build()\n586\t        .unwrap();\n587\t\n588\t    let response = client\n589\t        .get(\"http://localhost:7701/health\")\n590\t        .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer dev-node-key\")\n591\t        .send()\n592\t        .await\n593\t        .unwrap();\n594\t\n595\t    assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 200);\n596\t}\n597\t\n598\t/// Test 9: Node failure with RF=2\n599\t///\n600\t/// This test requires the RF=2 docker-compose stack:\n601\t///   docker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev-rf2.yml up -d\n602\t///\n603\t/// The test:\n604\t/// 1. Indexes documents with RF=2 (each document replicated to both groups)\n605\t/// 2. Stops a Meilisearch node mid-test (docker stop)\n606\t/// 3. Verifies that searches still work using remaining replicas\n607\t/// 4. Restarts the node and verifies recovery\n608\t#[tokio::test]\n609\t#[ignore] // Run with: cargo test --test integration test_node_failure_rf2 -- --ignored\n610\tasync fn test_node_failure_rf2() {\n611\t    // Use port 7710 for RF=2 stack\n612\t    let client = HttpClient {\n613\t        client: reqwest::Client::builder()\n614\t            .timeout(Duration::from_secs(30))\n615\t            .connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(5))\n616\t            .build()\n617\t            .expect(\"Failed to create HTTP client\"),\n618\t        base_url: \"http://localhost:7710\".to_string(),\n619\t        master_key: MASTER_KEY.to_string(),\n620\t    };\n621\t\n622\t    // Create index\n623\t    let create_body = json!({\n624\t        \"uid\": \"node_failure_test\",\n625\t        \"primaryKey\": \"id\"\n626\t    });\n627\t\n628\t    let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes\", &create_body).await.unwrap();\n629\t    assert!(status == 202 || status == 200, \"Index creation failed: {}\", body);\n630\t\n631\t    let task_uid: u64 = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&body)\n632\t        .unwrap()\n633\t        .get(\"taskUid\")\n634\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n635\t        .unwrap();\n636\t\n637\t    wait_for_task(&client, \"node_failure_test\", task_uid, 30).await.unwrap();\n638\t\n639\t    // Index 100 documents\n640\t    let docs: Vec<serde_json::Value> = (0..100).map(|i| {\n641\t        json!({\n642\t            \"id\": i,\n643\t            \"title\": format!(\"Document {}\", i),\n644\t            \"group\": i % 2, // Track which group this document belongs to\n645\t        })\n646\t    }).collect();\n647\t\n648\t    let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes/node_failure_test/documents\", &json!(docs)).await.unwrap();\n649\t    assert_eq!(status, 202, \"Document indexing failed: {}\", body);\n650\t\n651\t    let task_uid: u64 = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&body)\n652\t        .unwrap()\n653\t        .get(\"taskUid\")\n654\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n655\t        .unwrap();\n656\t\n657\t    wait_for_task(&client, \"node_failure_test\", task_uid, 60).await.unwrap();\n658\t\n659\t    // Verify all documents are searchable\n660\t    let search_body = json!({\n661\t        \"q\": \"\",\n662\t        \"limit\": 100\n663\t    });\n664\t\n665\t    let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes/node_failure_test/search\", &search_body).await.unwrap();\n666\t    assert_eq!(status, 200, \"Search failed: {}\", body);\n667\t\n668\t    let response: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&body).unwrap();\n669\t    let hits = response.get(\"hits\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()).unwrap();\n670\t    assert_eq!(hits.len(), 100, \"Expected 100 documents before node failure\");\n671\t\n672\t    // Stop meili-1 (a node in replica group 0)\n673\t    // This simulates a node failure - RF=2 means we still have 1 replica in group 0\n674\t    let output = std::process::Command::new(\"docker\")\n675\t        .args(&[\"stop\", \"miroir-meili-1\"])\n676\t        .output()\n677\t        .expect(\"Failed to stop container\");\n678\t\n679\t    assert!(output.status.success(), \"Failed to stop meili-1: {:?}\", String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr));\n680\t\n681\t    // Wait for Miroir to detect the failure\n682\t    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(5)).await;\n683\t\n684\t    // Search should still work with degraded availability\n685\t    // Some documents may be missing if they were only on the failed node\n686\t    let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes/node_failure_test/search\", &search_body).await.unwrap();\n687\t    assert_eq!(status, 200, \"Search failed after node failure: {}\", node\n695\t    // should still be accessible via the other replica in group 1\n696\t    assert!(hits_after.len() > 0, \"Expected some results after node failure\");\n697\t\n698\t    // Restart the node\n699\t    let output = std::process::Command::new(\"docker\")\n700\t        .args(&[\"start\", \"miroir-meili-1\"])\n701\t        .output()\n702\t        .expect(\"Failed to start container\");\n703\t\n704\t    assert!(output.status.success(), \"Failed to start meili-1: {:?}\", String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr));\n705\t\n706\t    // Wait for the node to recover and Miroir to detect it\n707\t    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(10)).await;\n708\t\n709\t    // After recovery, all documents should be accessible again\n710\t    let (status, body) = Integration tests for Miroir with docker-compose.\n//!\n//! These tests run against a real Miroir + Meilisearch stack started via docker-compose.\n//! Tests cover:\n//! - Document round-trip (1000 docs)\n//! - Search coverage across all shards (unique-keyword test)\n//! - Facet aggregation\n//! - Offset/limit paging\n//! - Settings broadcast\n//! - Task polling\n//! - Node failure with RF=2\n//!\n//! Run with:\n//!   cargo test --test integration -- --test-threads=1\n//!\n//! Prerequisites:\n//!   docker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml up -d\n\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\n/// Base URL for Miroir API (from docker-compose)\nconst MIROIR_BASE_URL: &str = \"http://localhost:7700\";\n\n/// Master key for authentication (from dev-config.yaml)\nconst MASTER_KEY: &str = \"dev-key\";\n\n/// HTTP client for making requests\n#[derive(Clone)]\nstruct HttpClient {\n    client: reqwest::Client,\n    base_url: String,\n    master_key: String,\n}\n\nimpl HttpClient {\n    fn new() -> Self {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::builder()\n            .timeout(Duration::from_secs(30))\n            .connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(5))\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"Failed to create HTTP client\");\n\n        Self {\n            client,\n            base_url: MIROIR_BASE_URL.to_string(),\n            master_key: MASTER_KEY.to_string(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    async fn get(&self, path: &str) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", self.base_url, path);\n        let response = self.client\n            .get(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"GET {} failed: {}\", url, e))?;\n\n        let status = response.status().as_u16();\n        let body = response.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to read body: {}\", e))?;\n        Ok((status, body))\n    }\n\n    async fn post(&self, path: &str, body: &serde_json::Value) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", self.base_url, path);\n        let response = self.client\n            .post(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .header(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n            .json(body)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"POST {} failed: {}\", url, e))?;\n\n        let status = response.status().as_u16();\n        let body = response.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to read body: {}\", e))?;\n        Ok((status, body))\n    }\n\n    async fn patch(&self, path: &str, body: &serde_json::Value) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", self.base_url, path);\n        let response = self.client\n            .patch(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .header(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n            .json(body)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"PATCH {} failed: {}\", url, e))?;\n\n        let status = response.status().as_u16();\n        let body = response.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to read body: {}\", e))?;\n        Ok((status, body))\n    }\n\n    async fn delete(&self, path: &str) -> Result<(u16, String), String> {\n        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", self.base_url, path);\n        let response = self.client\n            .delete(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.master_key))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"DELETE {} failed: {}\", url, e))?;\n\n        let status = response.status().as_u16();\n        let body = response.text().await.map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to read body: {}\", e))?;\n        Ok((status, body))\n    }\n}\n\n/// Wait for a task to complete by polling the task endpoint\nasync fn wait_for_task(client: &HttpClient, index_uid: &str, task_uid: u64, timeout_secs: u64) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String> {\n    let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n    let timeout = Duration::from_secs(timeout_secs);\n\n    while start.elapsed() < timeout {\n        let (status, body) = client.get(&format!(\"/tasks/{}\", task_uid)).await?;\n        if status == 200 {\n            if let Ok(task) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&body) {\n                if let Some(status_str) = task.get(\"status\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n                    if status_str == \"succeeded\" {\n                        return Ok(task);\n                    } else if status_str == \"failed\" {\n                        return Err(format!(\"Task failed: {}\", body));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n    }\n\n    Err(format!(\"Task {} timed out after {} seconds\", task_uid, timeout_secs))\n}\n\n/// Generate test documents with unique keywords for shard coverage testing\nfn generate_test_documents(count: usize) -> Vec<serde_json::Value> {\n    (0..count).map(|i| {\n        json!({\n            \"id\": i,\n            \"title\": format!(\"Document {}\", i),\n            \"keyword\": format!(\"keyword_{}\", i % 16), // 16 unique keywords for 16 shards\n            \"color\": [\"red\", \"green\", \"blue\"][i % 3], // For facet testing\n            \"score\": i % 100,\n            \"shard_hint\": format!(\"shard_{}\", i % 16), // Help verify shard distribution\n        })\n    }).collect()\n}\n\n/// Test 1: Document round-trip (1000 docs)\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_document_round_trip() {\n    let client = HttpClient::new();\n\n    // Create index\n    let create_body = json!({\n        \"uid\": \"round_trip_test\",\n        \"primaryKey\": \"id\"\n    });\n\n    let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes\", &create_body).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(status == 202 || status == 200, \"Index creation failed: {}\", body);\n\n    let task_uid: u64 = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&body)\n        .unwrap()\n        .get(\"taskUid\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n        .unwrap();\n\n    wait_for_task(&client, \"round_trip_test\", task_uid, 30).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Index 1000 documents\n    let docs = generate_test_documents(1000);\n    let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes/round_trip_test/documents\", &json!(docs)).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(status, 202, \"Document indexing failed: {}\", body);\n\n    let task_uid: u64 = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&body)\n        .unwrap()\n        .get(\"taskUid\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n        .unwrap();\n\n    wait_for_task(&client, \"round_trip_test\", task_uid, 60).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Verify all 1000 documents are retrievable by ID\n    for i in 0..1000 {\n        let (status, body) = client.get(&format!(\"/indexes/round_trip_test/documents/{}\", i)).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(status, 200, \"Failed to fetch document {}: {}\", i, body);\n\n        let doc: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&body).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(doc.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_i64()), Some(i64::try_from(i).unwrap()));\n        assert_eq!(doc.get(\"title\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()), Some(format!(\"Document {}\", i).as_str()));\n    }\n\n    // Clean up\n    let _ = client.delete(\"/indexes/round_trip_test\").await;\n}\n\n/// Test 2: Search covers all shards (unique-keyword test)\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_search_shard_coverage() {\n    let client = HttpClient::new();\n\n    // Create index\n    let create_body = json!({\n        \"uid\": \"shard_coverage_test\",\n        \"primaryKey\": \"id\"\n    });\n\n    let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes\", &create_body).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(status == 202 || status == 200, \"Index creation failed: {}\", wait_for_task(&client, \"shard_coverage_test\", task_uid, 30).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Configure filterable attributes to enable shard filtering\n    let settings_body = json!({\n        \"filterableAttributes\": [\"keyword\", \"shard_hint\", \"color\"]\n    });\n\n    let (status, body) = client.patch(\"/indexes/shard_coverage_test/settings\", &settings_body).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(status == 202 || status == 200, \"Settings update failed: {}\", body);\n\n    let task_uid: u64 = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&body)\n        .unwrap()\n        .get(\"taskUid\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n        .unwrap();\n\n    wait_for_task(&client, \"shard_coverage_test\", task_uid, 30).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Index documents\n    let docs = {}\", body);\n\n    let task_uid: u64 = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&body)\n        .unwrap()\n        .get(\"taskUid\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n        .unwrap();\n\n    wait_for_task(&client, \"shard_coverage_test\", task_uid, 60).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Search for each unique keyword and verify we find all documents with that keyword\n    let mut found_keywords: HashSet<String> = HashSet::new();\n\n    for shard_id in 0..16 {\n        let keyword = format!(\"keyword_{}\", shard_id);\n        let search_body = json!({\n            \"q\": keyword,\n            \"filter\": format!(\"keyword = {}\", keyword)\n        });\n\n        let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes/shard_coverage_test/search\", &search_body).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(status, 200, \"Search failed for keyword {}: {}\", keyword, body);\n\n        let response: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&body).unwrap();\n        let hits = response.get(\"hits\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()).unwrap();\n\n        // Each keyword should appear in ~62-63 documents (1000 / 16)\n        assert!(hits.len() >= 60 && hits.len() <= 65,\n            \"Expected ~62 documents for keyword {}, got {}\", keyword, hits.len());\n\n        found_keywords.insert(keyword);\n    }\n\n    // Verify we found all 16 keywords\n    assert_eq!(found_keywords.len(), 16, \"Expected to find all 16 keywords, found {}\", found_keywords.len());\n\n    // Clean up\n    let _ = client.delete(\"/indexes/shard_coverage_test\").await;\n}\n\n/// Test 3: Facet aggregation (3 colors, sum = 100)\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_facet_aggregation() {\n    let client =           \"color\": [\"red\", \"green\", \"blue\"][i % 3],\n        })\n    }).collect();\n\n    let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes/facet_test/documents\", &json!(docs)).await.unwrap();\n .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n        .unwrap();\n\n    wait_for_task(&client, \"facet_test\", task_uid, 30).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Search with facet distribution\n    let search_body = json!({\n        \"q\": \"\",\n        \"facets\": [\"color\"]\n    });\n\n    let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes/facet_test/search\", &search_body).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(status, 200, \"Facet search failed: {}\", body);\n\n    let response: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&body).unwrap();\n    let facet_distribution = response.get(\"facetDistribution\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_object())\n        .and_then(|o| o.get(\"color\"))\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_object())\n        .expect(\"No facet distribution found\");\n\n    let red_count = facet_distribution.get(\"red\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(0);\n    let green_count = facet_distribution.get(\"green\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(0);\n    let blue_count = facet_distribution.get(\"blue\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(0);\n\n    let total = red_count + green_count + blue_count;\n    assert_eq!(total, 100, \"Expected total of 100 documents across colors, got {}\", total);\n\n    // Each color should have approximately equal distribution (33-34 each)\n    assert!((32..=35).contains(&red_count), \"Expected ~33 red documents, got {}\", red_count);\n    assert!((32..=35).contains(&green_count), \"Expected ~33 green documents, got {}\", green_count);\n    assert!((32..=35).contains(&blue_count), \"Expected ~33 blue documents, got {}\", blue_count);\n\n    // Clean up\n    let _ = client.delete(\"/indexes/facet_test\").await;\n}\n\n/// Test 4: Offset/limit paging\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_offset_limit_paging() {\n    let {}\", i),\n        })\n    }).collect();\n\n    let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes/paging_test/documents\", &json!(docs)).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(status, 202, \"Document indexing failed: {}\", body);\n\n    let task_uid: u64 = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&body)\n        .unwrap()\n        .get(\"taskUid\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n        .unwrap();\n\n    wait_for_task(&client, \"paging_test\", task_uid, 30).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Test paging with offset and limit\n    let page_size = 20;\n    let mut all_ids: Vec<i64> = Vec::new();\n\n    for page in 0..5 {\n        let offset = page * page_size;\n        let search_body = json!({\n            \"q\": \"\",\n            \"limit\": page_size,\n            \"offset\": offset,\n            \"sort\": [\"id:asc\"]\n        });\n\n        let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes/paging_test/search\", &search_body).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(status, 200, \"Search failed for page {}: {}\", page, body);\n\n        let response: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&body).unwrap();\n        let hits = response.get(\"hits\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()).unwrap();\n\n        for hit in hits {\n            if let Some(id) = hit.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_i64()) {\n                all_ids.push(id);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Last page might have fewer results\n        let expected_count = if page < 4 { page_size } else { 0 };\n        if page < 4 {\n            assert_eq!(hits.len(), expected_count, \"Expected {} results on page {}, got {}\", expected_count, page, hits.len());\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify we got all 100 IDs and they're unique and sequential\n    assert_eq!(all_ids.len(), 100, \"Expected 100 total documents, got {}\", all_ids.len());\n    assert_eq!(all_ids, (0..100).collect::<Vec<_>>(), \"Documents are not in correct order\");\n\n    // Clean up\n    let _ = client.delete(\"/indexes/paging_test\").await;\n}\n\n/// Test 5: Settings broadcast\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_settings_broadcast() {\n    let client = HttpClient::new();\n\n    // Create index\n \"task_polling_test\", task_uid, 30).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(task.get(\"status\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()), Some(\"succeeded\"));\n    assert_eq!(task.get(\"type\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()), Some(\"documentAdditionOrUpdate\"));\n\n    // Clean up\n    let _ = client.delete(\"/indexes/task_polling_test\").await;\n}\n\n/// Test 7: Health check\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_health_check() {\n    let client = HttpClient::new();\n\n    let (status, body) = client.get(\"/health\").await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(status, 200, \"Health check failed: {}\", body);\n\n    let health: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&body).unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(health.get(\"status\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()), Some(\"available\"));\n}\n\n/// Test 8: Direct Meilisearch node access (for debugging)\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_direct_meilisearch_access() {\n    let client = HttpClient::new();\n\n    // Access Meilisearch node 0 directly\n    let client = reqwest::Client::builder()\n        .timeout(Duration::from_secs(5))\n        .build()\n        .unwrap();\n\n    let response = client\n        .get(\"http://localhost:7701/health\")\n        .header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer dev-node-key\")\n        .send()\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 200);\n}\n\n/// Test 9: Node failure with RF=2\n///\n/// This test requires the RF=2 docker-compose stack:\n///   docker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev-rf2.yml up -d\n///\n/// The test:\n/// 1. Indexes documents with RF=2 (each document replicated to both groups)\n/// 2. Stops a Meilisearch node mid-test (docker stop)\n/// 3. Verifies that searches still work using remaining replicas\n/// 4. Restarts the node and verifies recovery\n#[tokio::test]\n#[ignore] // Run with: cargo test --test integration test_node_failure_rf2 -- --ignored\nasync fn test_node_failure_rf2() {\n    // Use port 7710 for RF=2 stack\n    let client = HttpClient {\n        client: reqwest::Client::builder()\n            .timeout(Duration::from_secs(30))\n            .connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(5))\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"Failed to create HTTP client\"),\n        base_url: \"http://localhost:7710\".to_string(),\n        master_key: MASTER_KEY.to_string(),\n    };\n\n    // Create index\n    let create_body = json!({\n        \"uid\": \"node_failure_test\",\n        \"primaryKey\": \"id\"\n    });\n\n    let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes\", &create_body).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(status == 202 || status == 200, \"Index creation failed: {}\", body);\n\n    let task_uid: u64 = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&body)\n        .unwrap()\n        .get(\"taskUid\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n        .unwrap();\n\n    wait_for_task(&client, \"node_failure_test\", task_uid, 30).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Index 100 documents\n    let docs: Vec<serde_json::Value> = (0..100).map(|i| {\n        json!({\n            \"id\": i,\n            \"title\": format!(\"Document {}\", i),\n            \"group\": i % 2, // Track which group this document belongs to\n        })\n    }).collect();\n\n    let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes/node_failure_test/documents\", &json!(docs)).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(status, 202, \"Document indexing failed: {}\", body);\n\n    let task_uid: u64 = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&body)\n        .unwrap()\n        .get(\"taskUid\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n        .unwrap();\n\n    wait_for_task(&client, \"node_failure_test\", task_uid, 60).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Verify all documents are searchable\n    let search_body = json!({\n        \"q\": \"\",\n        \"limit\": 100\n    });\n\n    let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes/node_failure_test/search\", &search_body).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(status, 200, \"Search failed: {}\", body);\n\n    let response: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&body).unwrap();\n    let hits = response.get(\"hits\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()).unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(hits.len(), 100, \"Expected 100 documents before node failure\");\n\n    // Stop meili-1 (a node in replica group 0)\n    // This simulates a node failure - RF=2 means we still have 1 replica in group 0\n    let output = std::process::Command::new(\"docker\")\n        .args(&[\"stop\", \"miroir-meili-1\"])\n        .output()\n        .expect(\"Failed to stop container\");\n\n    assert!(output.status.success(), \"Failed to stop meili-1: {:?}\", String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr));\n\n    // Wait for Miroir to detect the failure\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(5)).await;\n\n    // Search should still work with degraded availability\n    // Some documents may be missing if they were only on the failed node\n    let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes/node_failure_test/search\", &search_body).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(status, 200, \"Search failed after node failure: {}\", body);\n\n    let response: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&body).unwrap();\n    let hits_after = response.get(\"hits\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()).unwrap();\n\n    // With RF=2 and 1 node down, we should still get results\n    // (each document has 2 replicas, 1 in each group)\n    // Since we stopped a node in group 0, documents with replicas on that node\n    // should still be accessible via the other replica in group 1\n    assert!(hits_after.len() > 0, \"Expected some results after node failure\");\n\n    // Restart the node\n    let output = std::process::Command::new(\"docker\")\n        .args(&[\"start\", \"miroir-meili-1\"])\n        .output()\n        .expect(\"Failed to start container\");\n\n    assert!(output.status.success(), \"Failed to start meili-1: {:?}\", String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr));\n\n    // Wait for the node to recover and Miroir to detect it\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(10)).await;\n\n    // After recovery, all documents should be accessible again\n    let (status, body) = client.post(\"/indexes/node_failure_test/search\", &search_body).await.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(status, 200, \"Search failed after node recovery: {}\", body);\n\n    let response: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&body).unwrap();\n    let hits_recovered = response.get(\"hits\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()).unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(hits_recovered.len(), 100, \"Expected all 100 documents after node recovery\");\n\n    // Clean up\n    let _ = client.delete(\"/indexes/node_failure_test\").await;\n}\n","numLines":720,"startLine":1,"totalLines":720}}}

These are intended for development, testing, and onboarding — not for production deployments.\n4\t\n5\t## Quick Start (5 minutes)\n6\t\n7\tStart the development stack with 3 Meilisearch nodes and one Miroir orchestrator:\n8\t\n9\t```bash\n10\t# From the repository root\n11\tdocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml up -d\n12\t\n13\t# Verify health\n14\tcurl http://localhost:7700/health\n15\t# Expected: {\"status\":\"available\"}\n16\t\n17\t# Index documents (Meilisearch-compatible API)\n18\tcurl -X POST http://localhost:7700/indexes/movies/documents \\\n19\t  -H \"Authorization: Bearer dev-key\" \\\n20\t  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n21\t  -d '[{\"id\": 1, \"title\": \"Inception\"}, {\"id\": 2, \"title\": \"Interstellar\"}]'\n22\t\n23\t# Search\n24\tcurl -X POST http://localhost:7700/indexes/movies/search \\\n25\t  -H \"Authorization: Bearer dev-key\" \\\n26\t  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n27\t  -d '{\"q\": \"inception\"}'\n28\t\n29\t# Teardown (removes containers and volumes)\n30\tdocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml down -v\n31\t```\n32\t\n33\t## Architecture\n34\t\n35\tThe development stack (`docker-compose-dev.yml`) consists of:\n36\t\n37\t| Service | Container Name | Port | Purpose |\n38\t|---------|---------------|------|---------|\n39\t| miroir | miroir-orchestrator | 7700 | Miroir orchestrator (client-facing API) |\n40\t| meili-0 | miroir-meili-0 | 7701 | Meilisearch node 0 (shard replica group 0) |\n41\t| meili-1 | miroir-meili-1 | 7702 | Meilisearch node 1 (shard replica group 0) |\n42\t| meili-2 | miroir-meili-2 | 7703 | Meilisearch node 2 (shard replica group 0) |\n43\t| redis | miroir-redis | 6379 | Optional: Task store for multi-replica deployments |\n44\t\n45\t### Sharding Configuration\n46\t\n47\tThe default `dev-config.yaml` configures:\n48\t- **16 logical shards** striped across 3 nodes\n49\t- **Replication factor: 1** (no redundancy; use RF≥2 for production)\n50\t- **1 replica group** (all nodes in the same failure domain)\n51\t- **Task store: SQLite** (use Redis for multi-replica deployments)\n52\t\n53\t## Multi-Replica Setup with Redis\n54\t\n55\tFor testing multi-replica deployments (RF≥2), enable Redis:\n56\t\n57\t1. Uncomment the `redis` service in `docker-compose-dev.yml`\n58\t2. Update `dev-config.yaml` to use Redis:\n59\t\n60\t```yaml\n61\ttask_store:\n62\t  backend: redis\n63\t  url: \"redis://redis:6379\"\n64\t```\n65\t\n66\t3. Increase replication factor:\n67\t\n68\t```yaml\n69\treplication_factor: 2\n70\t```\n71\t\n72\t4. Restart the stack:\n73\t\n74\t```bash\n75\tdocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml down -v\n76\tdocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml up -d\n77\t```\n78\t\n79\t## Configuration\n80\t\n81\tThe Miroir orchestrator is configured via `dev-config.yaml`, which is mounted read-only into the container at `/etc/miroir/config.yaml`. Key settings:\n82\t\n83\t| Setting | Value | Description |\n84\t|---------|-------|-------------|\n85\t| `master_key` | `dev-key` | Client API key (use for local testing) |\n86\t| `node_master_key` | `dev-node-key` | Key Miroir uses to authenticate to Meilisearch nodes |\n87\t| `shards` | `16` | Number of logical shards |\n88\t| `replication_factor` | `1` | Replication factor (increase for redundancy) |\n89\t| `task_store.backend` | `sqlite` | Task store backend (`sqlite` for dev, `redis` for multi-replica) |\n90\t\n91\t## Direct Meilisearch Access\n92\t\n93\tYou can access individual Meilisearch nodes directly (useful for debugging):\n94\t\n95\t```bash\n96\t# Node 0\n97\tcurl http://localhost:7701/health\n98\t\n99\t# Node 1\n100\tcurl http://localhost:7702/health\n101\t\n102\t# Node 2\n103\tcurl http://localhost:7703/health\n104\t```\n105\t\n106\t**Note:** Direct writes to Meilisearch nodes bypass Miroir's shard routing and are **not recommended**. Always write through the Miroir orchestrator.\n107\t\n108\t## Logs\n109\t\n110\tView logs for all services:\n111\t\n112\t```bash\n113\tdocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml logs -f\n114\t```\n115\t\n116\tView logs for a specific service:\n117\t\n118\t```bash\n119\tdocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml logs -f miroir\n120\tdocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml logs -f meili-0\n121\t```\n122\t\n123\t## Troubleshooting\n124\t\n125\t### Containers not starting\n126\t\n127\t```bash\n128\t# Check container status\n129\tdocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml ps\n130\t\n131\t# Check logs for errors\n132\tdocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml logs\n133\t```\n134\t\n135\t### Health check failing\n136\t\n137\t```bash\n138\t# Wait for containers to become healthy (can take 30-60 seconds)\n139\tdocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml ps\n140\t\n141\t# If health checks fail, check individual node health\n142\tcurl http://localhost:7701/health\n143\tcurl http://localhost:7702/health\n144\tcurl http://localhost:7703/health\n145\t```\n146\t\n147\t### Port conflicts\n148\t\n149\tIf ports 7700-7703 are already in use, modify the port mappings in `docker-compose-dev.yml`:\n150\t\n151\t```yaml\n152\tports:\n153\t  - \"7700:7700\"  # Change to \"7710:7700\" if 7700 is in use\n154\t```\n155\t\n156\t### Reset everything\n157\t\n158\t```bash\n159\t# Stop and remove all containers and volumes\n160\tdocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml down -v\n161\t\n162\t# Restart from scratch\n163\tdocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml up -d\n164\t```\n165\t\n166\t## Production Deployment\n167\t\n168\tFor production deployments on Kubernetes, use the [Miroir Helm chart](https://github.com/jedarden/miroir/tree/main/charts/miroir). See the main README.md for production deployment instructions.\n169\t\n170\t## CI/CD\n171\t\n172\tThe Docker Compose stack is exercised by CI smoke tests on every PR. These are intended for development, testing, and onboarding — not for production deployments.\n\n## Quick Start (5 minutes)\n\nStart the development stack with 3 Meilisearch nodes and one Miroir orchestrator:\n\n```bash\n# From the repository root\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml up -d\n\n# Verify health\ncurl http://localhost:7700/health\n# Expected: {\"status\":\"available\"}\n\n# Index documents (Meilisearch-compatible API)\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:7700/indexes/movies/documents \\\n  -H \"Authorization: Bearer dev-key\" \\\n  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  -d '[{\"id\": 1, \"title\": \"Inception\"}, {\"id\": 2, \"title\": \"Interstellar\"}]'\n\n# Search\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:7700/indexes/movies/search \\\n  -H \"Authorization: Bearer dev-key\" \\\n  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  -d '{\"q\": \"inception\"}'\n\n# Teardown (removes containers and volumes)\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml down -v\n```\n\n## Architecture\n\nThe development stack (`docker-compose-dev.yml`) consists of:\n\n| Service | Container Name | Port | Purpose |\n|---------|---------------|------|---------|\n| miroir | miroir-orchestrator | 7700 | Miroir orchestrator (client-facing API) |\n| meili-0 | miroir-meili-0 | 7701 | Meilisearch node 0 (shard replica group 0) |\n| meili-1 | miroir-meili-1 | 7702 | Meilisearch node 1 (shard replica group 0) |\n| meili-2 | miroir-meili-2 | 7703 | Meilisearch node 2 (shard replica group 0) |\n| redis | miroir-redis | 6379 | Optional: Task store for multi-replica deployments |\n\n### Sharding Configuration\n\nThe default `dev-config.yaml` configures:\n- **16 logical shards** striped across 3 nodes\n- **Replication factor: 1** (no redundancy; use RF≥2 for production)\n- **1 replica group** (all nodes in the same failure domain)\n- **Task store: SQLite** (use Redis for multi-replica deployments)\n\n## Multi-Replica Setup with Redis\n\nFor testing multi-replica deployments (RF≥2), enable Redis:\n\n1. Uncomment the `redis` service in `docker-compose-dev.yml`\n2. Update `dev-config.yaml` to use Redis:\n\n```yaml\ntask_store:\n  backend: redis\n  url: \"redis://redis:6379\"\n```\n\n3. Increase replication factor:\n\n```yaml\nreplication_factor: 2\n```\n\n4. Restart the stack:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml down -v\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml up -d\n```\n\n## Configuration\n\nThe Miroir orchestrator is configured via `dev-config.yaml`, which is mounted read-only into the container at `/etc/miroir/config.yaml`. Key settings:\n\n| Setting | Value | Description |\n|---------|-------|-------------|\n| `master_key` | `dev-key` | Client API key (use for local testing) |\n| `node_master_key` | `dev-node-key` | Key Miroir uses to authenticate to Meilisearch nodes |\n| `shards` | `16` | Number of logical shards |\n| `replication_factor` | `1` | Replication factor (increase for redundancy) |\n| `task_store.backend` | `sqlite` | Task store backend (`sqlite` for dev, `redis` for multi-replica) |\n\n## Direct Meilisearch Access\n\nYou can access individual Meilisearch nodes directly (useful for debugging):\n\n```bash\n# Node 0\ncurl http://localhost:7701/health\n\n# Node 1\ncurl http://localhost:7702/health\n\n# Node 2\ncurl http://localhost:7703/health\n```\n\n**Note:** Direct writes to Meilisearch nodes bypass Miroir's shard routing and are **not recommended**. Always write through the Miroir orchestrator.\n\n## Logs\n\nView logs for all services:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml logs -f\n```\n\nView logs for a specific service:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml logs -f miroir\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml logs -f meili-0\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Containers not starting\n\n```bash\n# Check container status\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml ps\n\n# Check logs for errors\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml logs\n```\n\n### Health check failing\n\n```bash\n# Wait for containers to become healthy (can take 30-60 seconds)\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml ps\n\n# If health checks fail, check individual node health\ncurl http://localhost:7701/health\ncurl http://localhost:7702/health\ncurl http://localhost:7703/health\n```\n\n### Port conflicts\n\nIf ports 7700-7703 are already in use, modify the port mappings in `docker-compose-dev.yml`:\n\n```yaml\nports:\n  - \"7700:7700\"  # Change to \"7710:7700\" if 7700 is in use\n```\n\n### Reset everything\n\n```bash\n# Stop and remove all containers and volumes\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml down -v\n\n# Restart from scratch\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml up -d\n```\n\n## Production Deployment\n\nFor production deployments on Kubernetes, use the [Miroir Helm chart](https://github.com/jedarden/miroir/tree/main/charts/miroir). See the main README.md for production deployment instructions.\n\n## CI/CD\n\nThe Docker Compose stack is exercised by CI smoke tests on every PR. See [k8s/argo-workflows/miroir-ci-docker-compose-smoke.yaml](../k8s/argo-workflows/miroir-ci-docker-compose-smoke.yaml) for the test workflow.\n\n## Integration Tests\n\nThis Docker Compose setup doubles as the integration test harness. Comprehensive integration tests are located in `crates/miroir-proxy/tests/docker_compose_integration.rs` with documentation in `crates/miroir-proxy/tests/README_integration.md`.\n\nRun integration tests:\n\n```bash\n# Start the stack\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml up -d\n\n# Run all integration tests\ncargo test -p miroir-proxy --test docker_compose_integration -- --test-threads=1\n\n# Clean up\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml down -v\n```\n\nIntegration test coverage includes:\n- Document round-trip (1000 documents)\n- Search coverage across all 16 shards\n- Facet aggregation\n- Offset/limit pagination\n- Settings broadcast\n- Task polling\n- Health checks\n- Node failure with RF=2 (requires `docker-compose-dev-rf2.yml`)\n","numLines":200,"startLine":1,"totalLines":200}}}

Integration Tests\n\nThis directory contains integration tests for Miroir that run against a real Docker Compose stack.\n\n## Prerequisites\n\nStart the Docker Compose development stack:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml up -d\n```\n\nWait for all containers to be healthy (may take 30-60 seconds):\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml ps\n```\n\n## Running Tests\n\nRun all integration tests:\n\n```bash\ncargo test -p miroir-proxy --test docker_compose_integration -- --test-threads=1\n```\n\nRun a specific test:\n\n```bash\ncargo test -p miroir-proxy --test docker_compose_integration test_document_round_trip -- --exact\n```\n\nRun tests with output:\n\n```bash\ncargo test -p miroir-proxy --test docker_compose_integration -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture\n```\n\n## Test Coverage\n\n| Test | Description | AC Reference |\n|------|-------------|--------------|\n| `test_document_round_trip` | Index and retrieve 1000 documents | §8.1 |\n| `test_search_shard_coverage` | Verify search hits all 16 shards | §8.2 |\n| `test_facet_aggregation` | Verify facet counts sum correctly | §8.3 |\n| `test_offset_limit_paging` | Test pagination with offset/limit | §8.4 |\n| `test_settings_broadcast` | Verify settings propagate to all nodes | §8.5 |\n| `test_task_polling` | Test task status polling | §8.6 |\n| `test_health_check` | Verify health endpoint responds | §8.7 |\n| `test_direct_meilisearch_access` | Verify direct node access for debugging | §8.7 |\n| `test_node_failure_rf2` | Test graceful degradation with node failure | §8.8 |\n\n## RF=2 / High Availability Tests\n\nThe `test_node_failure_rf2` test requires the RF=2 docker-compose stack:\n\n```bash\n# Start the RF=2 stack (6 Meilisearch nodes, RF=2, RG=2)\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev-rf2.yml up -d\n\n# Run the node failure test\ncargo test -p miroir-proxy --test docker_compose_integration test_node_failure_rf2 -- --ignored\n\n# Clean up\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev-rf2.yml down -v\n```\n\nThe node failure test:\n1. Indexes documents with RF=2 (each document replicated to both replica groups)\n2. Stops a Meilisearch node mid-test (`docker stop miroir-meili-1`)\n3. Verifies that searches still work using remaining replicas\n4. Restarts the node and verifies recovery\n\n## Cleanup\n\nStop and remove containers:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml down -v\n```\n\nFor RF=2 tests:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev-rf2.yml down -v\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Containers not starting\n\nCheck container status:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml ps\n```\n\nCheck logs:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml logs miroir\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml logs meili-0\n```\n\n### Port conflicts\n\nIf ports 7700-7703 are already in use, modify the port mappings in `examples/docker-compose-dev.yml`.\n\nFor RF=2 tests, ensure ports 7710-7716 and 6379 are available.\n\n### Tests failing with connection refused\n\nEnsure the Docker Compose stack is running:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose -f examples/docker-compose-dev.yml ps\n```\n\nWait for all containers to be healthy before running tests.\n\n### Node failure test not working\n\nThe RF=2 test requires:\n1. The RF=2 docker-compose stack to be running\n2. Sufficient memory for 6 Meilisearch containers + Redis\n3. The `docker` command to be accessible from the test environment\n\nRun with `--ignored` flag:\n```bash\ncargo test --test integration test_node_failure_rf2 -- --ignored\n```\n","numLines":132,"startLine":1,"totalLines":132}}}
